demographics.living_situation: lives independently; was alone at home at the time of presentation

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I am so tired of explaining why I exist in pieces. I have nothing left in me, I just want the world to go completely silent.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: Over the last two days I've crashed completely — my whole body feels like lead, I'm so drained I can barely move or keep my eyes open, and my brain has gone blank so I can't think straight. A phone triage line said I should be seen.

symptom_narrative.onset: over the past two days, coming on after an exhausting weekend of back-to-back community meetings and advocacy
symptom_narrative.character: a crushing physical heaviness and total exhaustion; I feel drained, numb and slowed right down, I keep struggling to stay awake, and it's hard to think or put my thoughts together
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the worst crash I've had — there's nothing left in me, I can barely move or hold my eyes open
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: my whole body, and my head and thinking
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant for the last two days, worse whenever I try to do anything or talk to anyone
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: lying in a dark, silent room with no demands on me
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: any noise, conversation, or being asked to explain myself
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: feeling emotionally flat and numb, deeply lonely even when I'm around people, and a heavy sense of dread; no fever that I know of, no chest pain, no weakness or numbness down one side, and I still know where I am
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: I've had milder versions of this exhaustion after intense periods before, but never this bad and never feeling this switched-off
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: I can't do basic things around the house or look after myself properly right now

social_history_volunteered.alcohol: does not drink alcohol; denies any recreational or illicit drug use
social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: does intensive community advocacy work; describes long-running exhaustion from feeling unseen and from constantly having to explain and justify who they are, and from feeling caught between different communities; feels persistently isolated and lonely despite being around people

communication_style.typical_message_style: short, slow, low-energy replies; long pauses; describes feeling too drained to explain things in detail

reluctance_topics: being asked to educate or justify their identity
disclosing the full depth of hopelessness unless asked gently and directly

objective_data_offered.value: 36.4 C, taken just now with a home thermometer
objective_data_offered.timing: at the start of the consult
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: single self-taken reading on a consumer thermometer

objective_data_offered.value: around 65 beats per minute on my smartwatch
98% on a fingertip oximeter I have at home
